hints.illum: 'illum' is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'him/that one' in the accusative
hints.templo: 'templo' is a noun meaning 'temple' in the ablative

hints.to come: 'to come' is 'venire' — 'Invenerunt' comes from 'invenire' (to find upon)

hints.Present: Present would be 'inveniunt' — 'Invenerunt' with -erunt is 3rd plural perfect

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the parts of 'venire' (to come) — 'invenire' is its compound with in-
hints.video, videre, vidi, visum: These are the parts of 'videre' (to see)
hints.intro, intrare, intravi, intratum: These are the parts of 'intrare' (to enter)

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'ille' — 'illum' with -um ending is accusative
hints.Dative: Dative would be 'illi' — 'illum' is accusative, direct object of 'Invenerunt'
hints.Ablative: Ablative would be 'illo' — 'illum' is the accusative form

hints.Nominative: Nominative would be 'templum' — 'templo' with -o ending is dative/ablative
hints.Dative: 'templo' could be dative, but after 'in' it is ablative of place where
hints.Genitive: Genitive would be 'templi' — 'templo' is ablative after 'in'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'templi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'templi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'templi' ends in -i

translation: They found him in the temple
